macro_markets.query: What are the most important macroeconomic and stock market news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include major index movements, Fed/central bank news, and significant company earnings or announcements.

items.title: Iran War Disrupts Global Supply Chains, Threatens AI Boom and Stock Markets
items.summary: The ongoing Iran war has closed the Strait of Hormuz, causing oil and gas shortages that are raising costs for electricity, semiconductors, and helium, potentially undermining the $1.5 trillion AI investment boom. Nasdaq-led stock markets have fallen about 10% from highs, entering correction territory, with OECD cutting 2026 global growth to 2.9%.

items.title: One-Year Anniversary of Trump's 'Liberation Day' Tariffs Highlights Economic Failures
items.summary: Marking April 2, 2025, as the anniversary of broad U.S. tariffs, manufacturing jobs dropped 90,000 since then, construction spending fell monthly in 2025, and trade uncertainty surged with over 50 tariff changes, accelerating global supply chain shifts away from U.S. reliance.

items.title: JPMorgan Strategist Warns of Inflation Spike to 3.5-4% from Oil and Tariffs
items.summary: David Kelly of JPMorgan Asset Management discusses how the Iran war and tariffs could push U.S. CPI inflation to 3.5% or nearly 4% year-over-year amid rising oil prices.

items.title: Former Commerce Secretary Ross Notes Consumers Feel Gas Price Inflation Pain
items.summary: Wilbur Ross highlights how U.S. inflation, particularly at the gas pump due to recent disruptions, is highly noticeable to consumers amid broader economic pressures.

crypto_news.query: What are the most significant cryptocurrency and blockchain news from the past 24 hours? Include major price movements, regulatory news, and notable project developments.

items.title: Crypto Market Experiences $443 Million in Liquidations
items.summary: The cryptocurrency market witnessed significant liquidations totaling $443 million in the past 24 hours, reflecting heightened market volatility and risk-off sentiment among traders.

items.title: Top Crypto Gainers Post Dramatic Gains Led by NTRN's 96% Surge
items.summary: NTRN skyrocketed 96.23% to $0.0035 with $6.11 million in trading volume, while CTSI gained 69.07% to $0.038 backed by $149.17 million in volume, indicating broad market participation in the rally.

items.title: Bitcoin Declines 2.4% Amid Geopolitical Tensions, Trading Near $66,852
items.summary: Bitcoin has dropped 1.8% over the past month and is trading near $66,852, down 46.9% from its October 2025 all-time high of $126,000, as institutional investors reduce exposure to volatile assets amid U.S.-Iran conflict.

items.title: Goldman Sachs Maintains Gold Bull Thesis Despite Recent Pullback
items.summary: Goldman Sachs reiterated its year-end gold price target of $5,400 per ounce, citing continued central bank buying and expected U.S. rate cuts, even as gold has shed 6.60% in the past month.

ai_news.query: What are the most important artificial intelligence and machine learning news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include new model releases, company announcements, research breakthroughs, and industry trends.

items.title: Arcee Releases Trinity-Large-Thinking Open Source AI Model
items.summary: Arcee has launched Trinity-Large-Thinking, an open-source U.S.-made AI model that enterprises can download and customize, offering an alternative to proprietary solutions.

items.title: OpenAI Acquires TBPN Business Talk Show Platform
items.summary: OpenAI has acquired TBPN, a founder-led business talk show, expanding its content and media presence.

items.title: Microsoft Launches Three New Foundational AI Models
items.summary: Microsoft has introduced three new foundational models to compete with other major AI providers in the rapidly evolving AI landscape.

items.title: OpenAI Brings ChatGPT Voice Mode to Apple CarPlay
items.summary: OpenAI has expanded ChatGPT's voice capabilities to Apple CarPlay, enabling voice-based AI interactions while driving.

items.title: Sony Gaming Division Acquires AI Startup for Photo-to-3D Technology
items.summary: Sony's gaming division has purchased an AI startup specializing in converting photos into 3D volumes, enhancing gaming and creative applications.

items.title: Google Expands AI Pro Plan Storage
items.summary: Google has increased storage capacity for its $20 per month AI Pro subscription plan, offering users more cloud storage alongside AI features.

items.title: FDA Approval Challenges for Depression-Detecting AI
items.summary: Regulatory hurdles continue to slow the approval of AI systems designed to detect depression, highlighting the complexity of bringing AI healthcare tools to market.

gaming.query: What are the most notable video game and gaming industry news from the past 24 hours? Include game releases, announcements, esports, and industry developments.

items.title: Last of Us Online Developer Reveals Game Was 80% Complete Before 'Soul-Crushing' Cancellation
items.summary: A Naughty Dog developer disclosed that the canceled Last of Us multiplayer game was around 80% complete before its cancellation nearly three years ago to focus on single-player narratives. The news highlights the emotional impact on the team.

items.title: Super Mario Galaxy Movie Breaks Records with Strong Opening Day
items.summary: The Super Mario Galaxy movie outperformed the first film's opening day but is expected to finish behind for the weekend. It stars Chris Pratt, Jack Black, and others from Universal and Illumination.

items.title: Sony Interactive Entertainment Acquires Cinemersive Labs
items.summary: Sony has made a new acquisition of Cinemersive Labs, expanding its portfolio in gaming and entertainment technologies.

items.title: LEGO Batman Legacy of the Dark Knight Gets Massive Update with Earlier Release Date
items.summary: LEGO Batman Legacy of the Dark Knight announced an earlier release: Standard Edition on May 22 and Deluxe on May 19, plus new suits like Batman '89 NES, Batgirl and Robin gameplay, and story teases.

items.title: The Witcher 4 Tech Demo Showcased at GDC 2026 with RTX Mega Geometry
items.summary: NVIDIA and CD Projekt RED demoed The Witcher 4 on RTX 5090 using Unreal Engine 5, RTX Mega Geometry, DLSS 4, and Path Tracing, achieving 80FPS at 4K. Ciri stars as a mutated Witcher with no release date yet.

items.title: Gray Zone Warfare 0.4 Update Brings Major Improvements
items.summary: Patch 0.4 for Gray Zone Warfare introduces significant enhancements, with testers reporting 40+ hours of playtesting focused on realizing the game's vision, potentially revitalizing the title.

items.title: Virtua Fighter 5 Receives Controversial Balance Patch Hinting at VF6
items.summary: The 20-year-old Virtua Fighter 5 got another balance update for VF5 R Evo World Stage, sparking controversy and speculation about upcoming changes for Virtua Fighter 6.

tech_news.query: What are the most important technology news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include major product launches, company news, and significant tech industry developments. Exclude AI-specific news.

items.title: NASA's Artemis II Moon Mission Launches Successfully
items.summary: NASA's Artemis II spacecraft launched as scheduled, beginning a 10-day, 600,000-mile mission with crew preparations underway. The mission marks a significant milestone in NASA's lunar exploration program.

items.title: Apple Removes Coding Apps from App Store, Blocks Replit Updates
items.summary: Apple pulled the 'Anything' coding app from its App Store and blocked Replit, a popular coding tool, from updating. The company cited enforcement of its app rules, though execution has been criticized as messy.

items.title: YouTube and Meta Ordered to Pay $6 Million in Landmark Damages Trial
items.summary: A jury decided that YouTube and Meta should pay $6 million in damages in a significant legal case, marking a landmark ruling in tech industry litigation.

items.title: Oracle Implements Layoffs Amid Massive AI Spending
items.summary: Oracle has laid off employees as the company pursues massive AI investments and works to cut costs in response to increased spending on artificial intelligence infrastructure.
